req.title: Export employee directory — variant 4
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct test dimensions: (1) happy-path CSV export with PII redaction for opted-out employees (EP – valid hr-admin role, mixed opt-in/opt-out employees); (2) 403 rejection for a non-hr-admin caller (NEG); (3) 429 rate-limit enforcement at the exact threshold boundary (BVA – one request over the limit); (4) decision-table coverage of the opt-out flag combinations (DT – all four combinations of two employees crossing opted-in/opted-out). Together these satisfy the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement and provide full condition coverage of the stated error pathways and variant semantics.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out
preconditions: At least one employee has opted IN to public directory (full PII visible)
At least one employee has opted OUT of public directory (PII must be redacted)
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain a valid bearer token via <get_token_fixture>
Seed or confirm existence of opted-in employee (id=E001) and opted-out employee (id=E002) via <seed_employees_fixture>
expected: Response body is valid CSV with a header row
Row for E001 contains full name and email
Row for E002 has name and email fields replaced with redaction marker (e.g. REDACTED)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv
import io

def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out(
    hr_admin_token,        # <fixture> returns bearer token string
    seed_employees,        # <fixture> seeds E001 opted-in, E002 opted-out
    api_client,            # <fixture> returns requests.Session with base URL
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
        stream=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["id"]: r for r in reader}

    assert "E001" in rows, "opted-in employee must appear"
    assert rows["E001"]["email"] not in ("", "REDACTED"), "full PII expected"

    assert "E002" in rows, "opted-out employee must appear in export"
    assert rows["E002"]["email"] == "REDACTED", "email must be redacted"
    assert rows["E002"]["name"] == "REDACTED", "name must be redacted"

tests.name: test_non_admin_export_returns_403
preconditions: Caller is authenticated but has role employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Authenticate as a regular employee and obtain token via <get_employee_token_fixture>
GET /api/employees/export.csv with the employee bearer token
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating insufficient permissions
trace: R-WEB-085: Error pathway – 403 if not hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_non_admin_export_returns_403(
    employee_token,   # <fixture> returns token for role:employee
    api_client,       # <fixture>
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "error payload expected"

tests.name: test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_at_boundary
preconditions: Rate-limit for /api/employees/export.csv is known (N requests per window)
Rate-limit counter for the test user is at zero at test start
actions: Reset rate-limit state for the token/user via <reset_rate_limit_fixture>
Send exactly N requests (the limit) and assert each returns 200
Send request N+1 (one over the boundary) and assert 429
expected: Request N+1 returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response for 429 includes Retry-After header or rate-limit error body
trace: BVA: test at limit N and one beyond limit N+1
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT = 5  # <configure> match server rate-limit window value

def test_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429_at_boundary(
    hr_admin_token,        # <fixture>
    api_client,            # <fixture>
    reset_rate_limit,      # <fixture> resets per-user counter before test
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    url = "/api/employees/export.csv"

    for i in range(1, RATE_LIMIT + 1):
        r = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"request {i} expected 200, got {r.status_code}"

    over_limit = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    has_retry = "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers
    has_body  = "error" in over_limit.text or "rate" in over_limit.text.lower()
    assert has_retry or has_body, "429 response must indicate rate-limit details"

tests.name: test_opt_out_combinations_decision_table
preconditions: Four employees exist: both opted-in, both opted-out, mixed (in/out), mixed (out/in)
actions: Seed four employees with ids E010-E013 covering all opt-out flag combinations via <seed_dt_employees_fixture>
Parse CSV and inspect each row's PII fields
expected: E010 opted_out=false: name and email are NOT REDACTED
E011 opted_out=true: name and email ARE REDACTED
E012 opted_out=false: name and email are NOT REDACTED
E013 opted_out=true: name and email ARE REDACTED
Total rows in CSV equals at least 4 (all seeded employees present)
trace: DT: all combinations of opted_out flag covered

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_export_returns_403
preconditions: No authentication credentials are provided in the request
actions: Send GET /api/employees/export.csv with no Authorization header
expected: No CSV data is returned in the response body
trace: R-WEB-085: Error pathway – 403 if not hr-admin
NEG: missing credentials treated as unauthorised caller
